questions.original_question: Whaat is the revenue stream of Zeekr at 2024?
questions.answer:
In 2024, Zeekr's revenue streams were primarily driven by vehicle sales, which accounted for RMB 55,315,306 (approximately USD 7,578,166), representing 72.9% of total revenues. Sales of batteries and other components contributed RMB 16,793,818 (approximately USD 2,300,744), making up 22.1% of total revenues. Research and development services and other services generated RMB 3,803,527 (approximately USD 521,081), accounting for 5.0% of total revenues. Overall, Zeekr's total revenue for 2024 was RMB 75,912,651 (approximately USD 10,399,991), reflecting significant growth across all categories.

This information is based on knowledge available before 2024-12-31.

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology's gross profit increased by 81.7% from RMB6,850.5 million in 2023 to RMB12,447.5 million (US$1,705.3 million) in 2024, which was primarily due to (i) an increase in gross profit from vehicle sales from RMB5,080.2 million in 2023 to RMB8,650.3 million (US$1,185.1 million) in 2024; (ii) an increase in gross profit from sales of batteries and other components from RMB884.5 million in 2023 to RMB2,312.7 million (US$316.8 million) in 2024; and (iii) an increase in gross profit from research and development services from RMB885.8 million in 2023 to RMB1,484.5 million (US$203.4 million) in 2024. Lotus Technology's gross profit margin increased from 13.3% in 2023 to 16.4% in 2024. The increase was mainly attributable to the slight growth of Lotus Technology's vehicle sales business, which had a higher gross profit margin at 15.6% in 2024. The gross profit and gross profit margin for the Zeekr segment in 2024 were RMB9,948.7 million (US$1,363.0 million) and 17.0%, respectively, compared with RMB5,791.9 million and 16.3% in 2023. The increase was primarily attributed to (i) the increase in delivery volume of Zeekr vehicles together with changes in pricing strategy to align with market dynamics and (ii) procurement savings due to the declining average costs in auto parts and other materials. For the Viridi segment, Lotus Technology recorded RMB954.2 million and RMB2,386.6 million (US$327.0 million) in gross profit and recorded gross profit margins of 5.2% and 13.3% in 2023 and 2024, respectively. The increase was primarily due to the growth in overseas sales of battery components, which had higher average gross margins.

title: CONSOLIDATED BALANCE SHEETS AS OF DECEMBER 31, 2023 AND 2024 (Amounts in thousands, except share and per share data and otherwise noted) 
summary: The consolidated balance sheets of Zeekr Intelligent Technology Holding Limited for the years ending December 31, 2023, and 2024, reveal significant changes in the company's asset structure. As of 2024, total assets increased from RMB 27,117,500 to RMB 32,670,720 (approximately US$ 4,475,868). Notably, cash and cash equivalents surged from RMB 3,260,670 to RMB 7,782,827 (US$ 1,066,243), while restricted cash and notes receivable also saw growth. However, inventories declined from RMB 5,228,689 to RMB 4,150,742. 

Current assets overall rose from RMB 20,477,108 to RMB 25,157,585 (US$ 3,446,575). In terms of non-current assets, property, plant, and equipment increased to RMB 3,225,287, although operating lease right-of-use assets fell to RMB 2,142,879, leading to a total increase in non-current assets from RMB 6,640,392 to RMB 7,513,135 (US$ 1,029,293). 

The financial statements, audited by Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu, emphasize warranty liabilities and the reliability of future claims forecasts. The accompanying notes provide critical insights that enhance the understanding of the financial position and performance of the company.

title: COMBINED AND CONSOLIDATED STATEMENTS OF CASH FLOWS (Continued) FOR THE YEARS ENDED DECEMBER 31, 2022, 2023 AND 2024 (Amounts in thousands, except share and per share data and otherwise noted) 
summary: The "Cash Flows from Financing Activities for the Years Ended December 31, 2022, 2023, and 2024" table provides a comprehensive analysis of Zeekr Intelligent Technology Holding Limited's financing activities across three fiscal years, detailing cash inflows and outflows from various sources, including public offerings, preferred shares, bank borrowings, and related party transactions, with amounts expressed in thousands of RMB and US dollars.

In 2024, the company successfully raised RMB 3,465,344 (US$ 474,750) from its initial public offering, marking a significant financial achievement, while no proceeds were recorded in the previous two years. The issuance of preferred shares was notably robust, generating RMB 1,268,360 in 2022 and RMB 5,373,044 in 2023, but saw no activity in 2024. Bank borrowings contributed RMB 147,000 in short-term inflows in 2022 and RMB 30,200 (US$ 4,137) in 2024, with long-term borrowings yielding RMB 972,042 in 2022 and RMB 414,480 (US$ 56,784) in 2024, although there were no borrowings in 2023.

The company faced significant repayments on both short-term and long-term borrowings, particularly in 2022 and 2024. Related party loans were substantial in 2022 at RMB 7,800,000 but declined to RMB 3,000,000 (US$ 410,998) by 2024, with notable repayments in the preceding years. The net cash flow from financing activities was positive in 2022 (RMB 5,373,325), turned negative in 2023 (RMB -2,683), and rebounded to a positive RMB 2,623,078 (US$ 359,360) in 2024.

Overall, the company's cash position improved significantly, transitioning from a decrease in 2022 to a substantial increase by the end of 2024, reaching RMB 8,961,652 (US$ 1,227,741). Additionally, cash payments for income tax and interest rose over the three years, indicating heightened financial activity. The table also highlights the effects of exchange rate fluctuations on cash balances and includes disclosures for non-cash investing and financing activities, such as accrued purchases of property, equipment, and intangible assets.

title: COMBINED AND CONSOLIDATED STATEMENTS OF OPERATIONS FOR THE YEARS ENDED DECEMBER 31, 2022, 2023 AND 2024 (Amounts in thousands, except share and per share data and otherwise noted) 
summary: The consolidated statements of operations for Zeekr Intelligent Technology Holding Limited for the fiscal years ending December 31, 2022, 2023, and 2024, illustrate the company's evolving financial landscape. Key metrics such as net revenues, costs, gross profit, operating expenses, and net losses are presented in RMB for the first two years and in USD for 2024.

Notable trends include a substantial increase in net revenues, which rose from RMB 31.9 billion in 2022 to RMB 75.9 billion in 2024, primarily driven by vehicle sales. Correspondingly, the cost of revenues also escalated, climbing from RMB 29.4 billion to RMB 63.5 billion over the same period, reflecting heightened expenses related to vehicle production and components. Despite these rising costs, gross profit improved significantly, increasing from RMB 2.5 billion in 2022 to RMB 12.4 billion in 2024.

Operating expenses grew from RMB 9.6 billion to RMB 18.9 billion, fueled by investments in research and development as well as selling, general, and administrative costs. Importantly, the net loss decreased from RMB 7.7 billion in 2022 to RMB 5.8 billion in 2024, indicating a positive shift in financial performance. The net loss per share showed a similar trend, with figures of RMB (3.97) for 2022, RMB (4.17) for 2023, and RMB (2.73) for 2024.

In summary, while Zeekr continues to face operating losses, the financial statements reveal a robust growth trajectory in revenues and gross profit margins, underscoring the company's commitment to its growth and development initiatives.

title: SEGMENT RESULTS OF OPERATION
summary: The report provides a detailed analysis of the financial performance of three operational segments—Zeekr, Viridi, and ZTE—structured according to the management's internal reporting framework for decision-making and performance evaluation. Each segment's operations are summarized, highlighting key metrics such as net revenues, costs of revenues, segment profits, and vehicle deliveries, with comprehensive data available in Note 23 of the annual report's combined financial statements.

In the **Zeekr Segment**, net revenues experienced significant growth, rising from RMB 20.63 billion in 2022 to RMB 35.61 billion in 2023, and reaching RMB 58.58 billion in 2024, primarily driven by increased vehicle sales. Correspondingly, costs of revenues rose sharply, resulting in a substantial increase in segment profit from RMB 1.04 billion in 2022 to nearly RMB 9.95 billion in 2024, alongside a notable increase in vehicle deliveries from 71,941 units in 2022 to 222,123 units in 2024.

The **Viridi Segment** saw net revenues grow from RMB 12.80 billion in 2022 to RMB 18.20 billion in 2023, before a slight decline to RMB 18.01 billion in 2024. While costs peaked in 2023, segment profit fluctuated, dropping to RMB 0.95 billion in 2023 before recovering to RMB 2.39 billion in 2024.

In the **ZTE Segment**, net revenues slightly decreased from RMB 2.44 billion in 2022 to RMB 2.26 billion in 2023, but showed a modest recovery to RMB 2.35 billion in 2024. Despite variations in costs, segment profit remained resilient, increasing from RMB 0.48 billion in 2022 to RMB 0.50 billion in 2024 after a dip in 2023.

Inter-segment transactions are also highlighted, with the Zeekr Segment providing R&D services to the Viridi Segment and vice versa, which are eliminated upon consolidation. The report underscores the interconnectedness of the segments and the financial implications of their transactions, while the accompanying table presents a comprehensive overview of their financial performance from 2022 to 2024, reflecting significant growth and variations across the years. title: 18. Revenue 
summary: The summary provides an overview of ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited's revenue recognition methods and financial performance over the years 2022 to 2024. It distinguishes between two key revenue recognition approaches: "Revenue recognized at a point in time," which occurs upon the delivery of goods or completion of services, and "Revenue recognized over time," which allows for gradual recognition as services are performed or benefits are realized. 

In terms of financial performance, ZEEKR reported total revenues of RMB 31,899,448 in 2022, which increased significantly to RMB 51,672,618 in 2023, and further rose to RMB 75,912,651 in 2024. This growth reflects the company's ability to generate income from both immediate sales and ongoing service contracts.

The summary also highlights the Group's contract liabilities, which include payments received from customers before the delivery of goods and services. As of December 31 for the respective years, current contract liabilities were RMB 288,282 in 2022, RMB 497,729 in 2023, and RMB 241,091 in 2024, while non-current liabilities were RMB 137,206, RMB 215,369, and RMB 62,724 for the same years. 

Additionally, the reconciliation of accounts receivable and contract liabilities shows fluctuations over the reporting periods, with accounts receivable increasing from RMB 24,208 at the start of 2022 to RMB 1,744,334 by the end of 2024, while contract liabilities experienced various changes throughout the years. By the end of 2024, the Group had unsatisfied performance obligations amounting to RMB 241,091, expected to be recognized as revenue within the following year. Overall, the summary captures the financial trends and variations in revenue and liabilities, illustrating the Group's strategic financial management and growth trajectory.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology's gross profit increased by 81.7% from RMB6,850.5 million in 2023 to RMB12,447.5 million (US$1,705.3 million) in 2024, which was primarily due to (i) an increase in gross profit from vehicle sales from RMB5,080.2 million in 2023 to RMB8,650.3 million (US$1,185.1 million) in 2024; (ii) an increase in gross profit from sales of batteries and other components from RMB884.5 million in 2023 to RMB2,312.7 million (US$316.8 million) in 2024; and (iii) an increase in gross profit from research and development services from RMB885.8 million in 2023 to RMB1,484.5 million (US$203.4 million) in 2024. Lotus Technology's gross profit margin increased from 13.3% in 2023 to 16.4% in 2024. The increase was mainly attributable to the slight growth of Lotus Technology's vehicle sales business, which had a higher gross profit margin at 15.6% in 2024. The gross profit and gross profit margin for the Zeekr segment in 2024 were RMB9,948.7 million (US$1,363.0 million) and 17.0%, respectively, compared with RMB5,791.9 million and 16.3% in 2023. The increase was primarily attributed to (i) the increase in delivery volume of Zeekr vehicles together with changes in pricing strategy to align with market dynamics and (ii) procurement savings due to the declining average costs in auto parts and other materials. For the Viridi segment, Lotus Technology recorded RMB954.2 million and RMB2,386.6 million (US$327.0 million) in gross profit and recorded gross profit margins of 5.2% and 13.3% in 2023 and 2024, respectively. The increase was primarily due to the growth in overseas sales of battery components, which had higher average gross margins.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Gross profit, gross profit margin 
summary: In 2024, the company experienced a substantial increase in gross profit, rising by 81.7% to reach RMB 12,447.5 million (approximately US$1,705.3 million), up from RMB 6,850.5 million in 2023. This growth was fueled by significant contributions from various segments: vehicle sales gross profit surged from RMB 5,080.2 million to RMB 8,650.3 million (US$1,185.1 million), while battery and component sales profits rose from RMB 884.5 million to RMB 2,312.7 million (US$316.8 million). Additionally, gross profit from research and development services increased from RMB 885.8 million to RMB 1,484.5 million (US$203.4 million). The overall gross profit margin improved from 13.3% in 2023 to 16.4% in 2024, largely due to a modest rise in vehicle sales, which achieved a higher margin of 15.6%.

Zeekr also reported strong performance, with gross profit climbing to RMB 9,948.7 million (approximately US$1,363.0 million) and a margin of 17.0%, up from RMB 5,791.9 million and 16.3% in the previous year. This growth was attributed to increased delivery volumes, strategic pricing adjustments, and cost efficiencies from lower auto parts prices. 

In the Viridi Segment, gross profit rose significantly from RMB 954.2 million in 2023 to RMB 2,386.6 million (approximately US$327.0 million) in 2024, with margins improving from 5.2% to 13.3%, driven by a boost in overseas battery component sales. The ZTE segment also saw notable growth, with gross profit increasing from RMB 265.1 million to RMB 501.7 million (approximately US$68.7 million) and margins enhancing from 11.7% to 21.3%, primarily due to higher sales of research and development services to related parties.

title: 18. Revenue 
summary: The summary provides an overview of ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited's revenue recognition methods and financial performance over the years 2022 to 2024. It distinguishes between two key revenue recognition approaches: "Revenue recognized at a point in time," which occurs upon the delivery of goods or completion of services, and "Revenue recognized over time," which allows for gradual recognition as services are performed or benefits are realized. 

In terms of financial performance, ZEEKR reported total revenues of RMB 31,899,448 in 2022, which increased significantly to RMB 51,672,618 in 2023, and further rose to RMB 75,912,651 in 2024. This growth reflects the company's ability to generate income from both immediate sales and ongoing service contracts.

The summary also highlights the Group's contract liabilities, which include payments received from customers before the delivery of goods and services. As of December 31 for the respective years, current contract liabilities were RMB 288,282 in 2022, RMB 497,729 in 2023, and RMB 241,091 in 2024, while non-current liabilities were RMB 137,206, RMB 215,369, and RMB 62,724 for the same years. 

Additionally, the reconciliation of accounts receivable and contract liabilities shows fluctuations over the reporting periods, with accounts receivable increasing from RMB 24,208 at the start of 2022 to RMB 1,744,334 by the end of 2024, while contract liabilities experienced various changes throughout the years. By the end of 2024, the Group had unsatisfied performance obligations amounting to RMB 241,091, expected to be recognized as revenue within the following year. Overall, the summary captures the financial trends and variations in revenue and liabilities, illustrating the Group's strategic financial management and growth trajectory.

title: Gross profit, gross profit margin 
summary: In 2023, the company experienced a remarkable financial turnaround, with gross profit soaring by 177.1% to reach RMB 6,850.5 million (approximately US$964.9 million), up from RMB 2,472.1 million in 2022. This surge was largely fueled by a significant increase in vehicle sales, which contributed to a gross profit margin rise from 7.7% to 13.3%. Revenue also saw a substantial boost, climbing from RMB 923.1 million to RMB 5,080.2 million (approximately US$715.5 million). 

While gross profit from battery and component sales declined from RMB 1,091.8 million to RMB 884.5 million (US$124.6 million), research and development services experienced a notable increase, rising to RMB 885.8 million (US$124.8 million) from RMB 457.2 million in 2022. ZEEKR reported a gross profit of RMB 5,791.9 million (approximately US$815.8 million) and a gross profit margin of 16.3%, significantly up from RMB 1,042.6 million and 5.1% the previous year, driven by higher vehicle delivery volumes and cost savings.

title: OUR VEHICLES 
summary: ZEEKR is strategically positioned in the premium battery electric vehicle (BEV) market, focusing on the design, engineering, and sale of advanced electric vehicles that enhance user experience and drivability. Leveraging its strong research and development capabilities and synergies with Geely Group, ZEEKR aims to tap into China's burgeoning premium BEV market, which is projected to grow significantly from 666,400 units in 2024 to 2,607,600 units by 2028, according to Frost & Sullivan.

Since its inception, ZEEKR has launched several models, starting with the ZEEKR 001 in 2021, followed by the upgraded 2024 model, the ZEEKR 009, and the compact SUV ZEEKR X. The company has also expanded its offerings with the ZEEKR 001 FR and an upscale sedan, reflecting its commitment to catering to diverse customer needs. As of December 31, 2023, ZEEKR has delivered a total of 196,633 vehicles, primarily in China, marking one of the fastest growth rates in the premium BEV segment.

The monthly delivery data from June 2023 to March 2024 shows a consistent upward trend, with March 2024 witnessing 13,012 units delivered. This growth is indicative of rising consumer demand and ZEEKR's operational capabilities, positioning the company as a significant player in the global market. ZEEKR has also begun deliveries in Europe and plans to supply vehicles for the Waymo One Fleet in the U.S., highlighting its ambitions for international expansion. Overall, ZEEKR's trajectory reflects a robust commitment to innovation and market growth in the premium electric vehicle sector.

Additionally, battery and component sales increased due to a favorable product mix and overseas sales, while R&D services also saw a boost from enhanced licensing and spare parts revenue.

title: SEGMENT RESULTS OF OPERATION
summary: The report provides a detailed analysis of the financial performance of three operational segments—Zeekr, Viridi, and ZTE—structured according to the management's internal reporting framework for decision-making and performance evaluation. Each segment's operations are summarized, highlighting key metrics such as net revenues, costs of revenues, segment profits, and vehicle deliveries, with comprehensive data available in Note 23 of the annual report's combined financial statements.

In the **Zeekr Segment**, net revenues experienced significant growth, rising from RMB 20.63 billion in 2022 to RMB 35.61 billion in 2023, and reaching RMB 58.58 billion in 2024, primarily driven by increased vehicle sales. Correspondingly, costs of revenues rose sharply, resulting in a substantial increase in segment profit from RMB 1.04 billion in 2022 to nearly RMB 9.95 billion in 2024, alongside a notable increase in vehicle deliveries from 71,941 units in 2022 to 222,123 units in 2024.

The **Viridi Segment** saw net revenues grow from RMB 12.80 billion in 2022 to RMB 18.20 billion in 2023, before a slight decline to RMB 18.01 billion in 2024. While costs peaked in 2023, segment profit fluctuated, dropping to RMB 0.95 billion in 2023 before recovering to RMB 2.39 billion in 2024.

In the **ZTE Segment**, net revenues slightly decreased from RMB 2.44 billion in 2022 to RMB 2.26 billion in 2023, but showed a modest recovery to RMB 2.35 billion in 2024. Despite variations in costs, segment profit remained resilient, increasing from RMB 0.48 billion in 2022 to RMB 0.50 billion in 2024 after a dip in 2023.

Inter-segment transactions are also highlighted, with the Zeekr Segment providing R&D services to the Viridi Segment and vice versa, which are eliminated upon consolidation. The report underscores the interconnectedness of the segments and the financial implications of their transactions, while the accompanying table presents a comprehensive overview of their financial performance from 2022 to 2024, reflecting significant growth and variations across the years. All figures are reported in RMB, with some values rounded to thousands.

title: 23. Segment reporting (Continued) 
summary: The summary provides a comprehensive overview of the financial performance and operational metrics of the Group's reportable segments—Zeekr, Viridi, and ZTE—for the fiscal years ending December 31, 2022, 2023, and 2024. 

### Financial Performance Overview

1. **Revenue Generation**:
   - **2022**: Consolidated revenues reached RMB 35,869,092, with Zeekr leading at RMB 20,630,041, followed by Viridi at RMB 12,799,124, and ZTE at RMB 2,439,927.
   - **2023**: Total consolidated revenue increased to RMB 56,076,975, primarily driven by Zeekr's external revenue of RMB 35,614,648 and Viridi's total of RMB 18,203,200.
   - **2024**: The trend continued with strong revenue contributions from all segments, particularly from vehicle sales in China.

2. **Gross Profit Analysis**:
   - The Group's total gross profit for 2022 was RMB 2,693,996, which increased to RMB 7,011,244 in 2023, reflecting improved operational efficiency and revenue growth across segments.

3. **Expenses**:
   - Research and Development (R&D) expenses were significant, peaking at RMB 5,446,320 in 2022 and decreasing to RMB 8,369,207 in 2023, indicating a shift in focus or efficiency improvements.
   - Selling, General, and Administrative (SG&A) expenses also showed a downward trend, from RMB 4,245,317 in 2022 to RMB 6,920,561 in 2023, highlighting cost management efforts.

4. **Consolidated Loss**:
   - The Group reported a consolidated loss before income tax of RMB 7,355,091 in 2022, which worsened to RMB (8,288,920) in 2023, reflecting the challenges faced despite revenue growth.

### Segment Insights

- **Zeekr Segment**: Demonstrated robust growth in both revenue and gross profit, becoming the primary driver of the Group's financial performance.
- **Viridi Segment**: Contributed significantly to intersegment revenues, particularly through sales of battery packs to Zeekr.
- **ZTE Segment**: Showed a decline in asset value and revenue, indicating potential strategic reevaluation.

### Geographic Revenue Breakdown

The Group's revenues were heavily concentrated in China, which accounted for the majority of total sales across all segments. In 2023, China generated RMB 51,672,618 in external revenue, while Europe and other regions contributed significantly less, underscoring the importance of the Chinese market.

### Asset Allocation

The analysis of long-term assets revealed a strategic focus on growth in the Zeekr segment, with total assets increasing from RMB 9,618,203 in 2022 to RMB 24,393,390 in 2024. In contrast, the Viridi and ZTE segments experienced fluctuations, indicating a potential shift in investment priorities.

### Conclusion

Overall, the summary highlights the Group's operational effectiveness, financial health, and strategic focus across its segments and geographic markets. The data underscores the importance of the Chinese market in driving revenue, while also reflecting ongoing challenges in managing expenses and achieving profitability.

The following table sets forth a breakdown of Lotus Technology's cost of revenues, expressed in the absolute amount and as a percentage of Lotus Technology's cost of revenues, for the periods indicated. From 2023 to 2024, Lotus Technology's cost of revenues increased by 41.6% from RMB44,822.1 million to RMB63,465.2 million (US$8,694.7 million), respectively. This was primarily due to: (i) Lotus Technology experienced an increase in the cost of revenues associated with vehicle sales in the Zeekr Segment from RMB28,831.6 million in 2023 to RMB46,665.1 million (US$6,393.1 million) in 2024, which is in line with more vehicles delivered in 2024, partially offset by the declining average costs in auto parts and other materials.  
(ii) Lotus Technology had an increase in the cost of revenues associated with sales of batteries and other components in the Viridi Segment from RMB13,808.1 million in 2023 to RMB14,481.1 million (US$1,983.9 million) in 2024, as Ningbo Viridi delivered more battery packs as well as electric drives.  
(iii) During the same periods, the cost of revenues associated with Lotus Technology's research and development services and other services mainly from the Zeekr Segment and ZTE Segment increased from RMB2,182.4 million to RMB2,319.1 million (US$317.7 million), which was consistent with the growth trend of vehicle spare parts.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Cost of revenues 
summary: The analysis of our cost of revenues reveals a substantial increase of 41.6% from 2023 to 2024, with total costs rising from RMB 44,822.1 million to RMB 63,465.2 million (around US$8,694.7 million). This growth is largely driven by the Zeekr Segment, where vehicle sales costs surged from RMB 28,831.6 million to RMB 46,665.1 million (US$6,393.1 million), fueled by higher vehicle deliveries, despite a reduction in average auto parts costs. The Viridi Segment also contributed to the overall increase, with costs for battery and component sales climbing from RMB 13,808.1 million to RMB 14,481.1 million (US$1,983.9 million) due to greater deliveries of battery packs and electric drives. Additionally, costs associated with research and development and other services from the Zeekr and ZTE Segments rose, although the exact figures for 2024 are not specified. Meanwhile, the vehicle spare parts sector demonstrated consistent growth, generating revenue of RMB 2,319.1 million (approximately US$317.7 million).
